name: RAG Systems
description: Designing retrieval-first assistants that answer from a controlled knowledge base instead of improvising unsupported claims.
proofExamples: AI Telegram Assistant uses company knowledge as the source of truth.
Portfolio Oracle is designed with static retrieval fallback before LLM generation.

name: Ollama / Local LLM
description: Running local models for private demos, internal assistants and offline-friendly AI experiments.
proofExamples: AI Telegram Assistant architecture includes Ollama/local model support.
Portfolio Oracle has a planned local Ollama provider adapter.

name: ChromaDB / Vector Store
description: Using vector storage concepts for semantic search and document retrieval in AI assistant pipelines.
proofExamples: AI Telegram Assistant uses ChromaDB as part of the RAG pipeline.

name: Prompt Engineering
description: Building constrained prompts with context, refusal rules and task-specific answer formats.
proofExamples: Portfolio Oracle has explicit no-hallucination and source-only answer rules.
Telegram assistant is designed to avoid answers outside the knowledge base.

name: Python Backend
description: Building backend services, automation workflows and AI pipeline logic in Python.
proofExamples: AI Telegram Assistant backend is Python-based.
CBDC research used Python data analysis and econometric workflow.

name: Django REST Framework
description: Building API-first backends with models, serializers, authentication and role-aware endpoints.
proofExamples: FinCat uses DRF endpoints for financial organization catalog CRUD.
FinCat includes JWT authentication and admin/user role separation.

name: PostgreSQL
description: Designing relational persistence for catalogs, assistant metadata and structured application data.
proofExamples: FinCat runs with PostgreSQL in Docker.
AI Telegram Assistant architecture includes PostgreSQL.

name: API Design
description: Designing endpoints around actual user roles, frontend needs and maintainable resource boundaries.
proofExamples: FinCat separates catalog access and admin management flows.
AbafarConvertor separates client experience from backend conversion service.

name: React
description: Building modular, component-driven interfaces with data separated from rendering logic.
proofExamples: Learning Clicker uses React for progression, events and upgrade UI.
Arcane Systems Dossier uses React + TypeScript with feature-based modules.

name: TypeScript
description: Using explicit types to keep portfolio data, project models and feature contracts maintainable.
proofExamples: Arcane Systems Dossier uses typed data models for projects, skills and AI providers.

name: Vue / Nuxt 3
description: Building frontend pages and admin flows with Nuxt 3 for full-stack catalog products.
proofExamples: FinCat frontend is implemented with Nuxt 3 and Vue 3.

name: Tailwind + Design System
description: Creating reusable UI components backed by tokens instead of one-off styling.
proofExamples: Arcane Systems Dossier uses tokens for colors, radii, spacing and theme direction.

name: Three.js / React Three Fiber
description: Creative 3D visualization direction for hero scenes, constellations and interactive data maps.
proofExamples: The portfolio architecture reserves isolated Three.js modules for hero and music constellation scenes.

name: Docker
description: Packaging frontend, backend and database services into reproducible local environments.
proofExamples: FinCat includes Docker and Docker Compose packaging for frontend, backend and database.

name: Static Deployment
description: Designing web apps that can be deployed as static files without a permanently running backend.
proofExamples: Arcane Systems Dossier is built around static-first deployment and progressive AI enhancement.

name: OLS / Econometrics
description: Applying regression analysis, diagnostics and scenario interpretation to macro-financial questions.
proofExamples: CBDC research used OLS, robust standard errors, VIF and residual diagnostics.

name: Financial Analytics
description: Connecting technical analysis with financial systems, central banking context and product implications.
proofExamples: Master's background in Applied Finance / Financial Analytics.
FinCat and PBI Monitor are connected to financial/business analytics workflows.

name: Dashboard Monitoring
description: Designing workflows to track report freshness, update status and business analytics reliability.
proofExamples: PBI Monitor automates daily Power BI dashboard refresh control.

name: Product Thinking
description: Turning workflow pain points into focused tools with clear user value and future extension paths.
proofExamples: PBI Monitor is positioned as a practical internal productivity product, not just a script.
Arcane Systems Dossier turns a resume into a product-like interactive system.

name: Game UI / Progression Design
description: Designing interactive progression loops, upgrades, events and feedback systems for engaging interfaces.
proofExamples: Learning Clicker includes upgrades, eras, events, prestige and achievement-style interactions.

name: Research Methodology
description: Structuring hypotheses, methods, limitations and conclusions around evidence instead of vague claims.
proofExamples: CBDC thesis used defined variables, diagnostics and scenario analysis.
Portfolio case studies are structured around problem, solution, role and result.
